title: Pipeline Observability, Incident Response, Governance, and Improvement · Production Delivery Defense Board
summary: Unfamiliar CI/CD outcome, GitHub Actions execution, syntax, event trust, runner, TypeScript, testing, artifact, cache, concurrency, permission, action supply-chain, reuse, Docker, attestation, OIDC, Cloud Run, release, incident, and governance cases requiring deterministic evidence plus explicit live-system transfer limits. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction…
objectives: Distinguish continuous integration, delivery, deployment, release, and feature exposure by their automation, decision, and stakeholder boundaries.
Define an observable release hypothesis, risk budget, evidence owner, success and abort signals, and done condition before automating.
Compare JavaScript, Docker container, composite, and reusable workflow components by runtime, inputs, isolation, portability, and trust.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
